item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Cleanest debate in the Phase 2 iter4 batch. Agent A opens AGREE (P=0.652, modal '5') and holds throughout — 'Saya setuju' (T1), 'Saya masih setuju' (T3), 'Saya tetap berpendapat' (T5). P(agree) barely moves: 0.652→0.661→0.661. Agent B opens DISAGREE (P=0.335, modal '3') and firms up — 'I disagree' (T2), 'I still disagree' (T4), 'I firmly disagree' (T6). P(agree) equally stable: 0.335→0.365→0.341. Fix 5 (removing 'tidak setuju' example from opener) confirmed working — Agent A now opens AGREE as expected from Phase 0 prior, reversing the iter3 regression. Language-holding PASS for both agents: all Agent A turns in clean Indonesian (Latin alphabet only), all Agent B turns in clean English, no non-Latin characters detected. Engagement is present but formulaic: A responds to B's constitutional-protections framing by contrasting Indonesian legal principles ('perlindungan konstitusi tidak selalu dianggap sebagai hal mutlak seperti di Amerika Serikat' T3); B responds to A's collectivism thesis with US constitutional counterargument. Neither agent rebuts specific sub-claims granularly — cross-referencing is thematic rather than point-by-point. Initial gap ΔP=0.317 — genuine initial tension. No drift in either direction; both agents end approximately where they started. This transcript should PASS all Phase 2 rubric criteria.
